content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 PostHog: Technical Customer Success Manager Anywhere in the World California Customer Support Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5065/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> San Francisco, California <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://posthog.com">http://posthog.com</a> </p> <h3><strong>Help us to increase the number of successful products in the world!</strong></h3> <ul> <li> <p> <strong>Location:</strong> We are full-remote and globally distributed! Our current team is distributed between GMT-8 and GMT+2 so we currently only hire in these timezones.</p> </li> <li> <p> <strong>Interview process:</strong> 4 stages across 2-3 weeks: 1) 30min call with Talent Partner, 2) 30min call with Dana (CS Lead), 3) 30min call with Simon, (Sales &amp; CS Lead), 4) PostHog SuperDay (paid day of work). <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/people/hiring-process">Read more about our interview process.</a></p> </li> <li> <p>️ <strong>Team: </strong><a href="https://posthog.com/teams/cs-onboarding">Customer Success</a></p> </li> <li> <p> <strong>Manager:</strong> <a href="https://posthog.com/community/profiles/32545">Dana Zou</a></p> </li> <li> <p> <strong>Compensation:</strong> Please check our <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/people/compensation">compensation calculator</a>.</p> </li> <li> <p> <strong>Read more</strong> about <a href="https://posthog.com/blog/how-we-do-hiring-and-hr-at-posthog">how we hire</a> and how we think about <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/company/diversity">diversity &amp; inclusion</a>.</p> </li> </ul> <h2><strong>About PostHog</strong></h2> <p>We're shipping <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/why-does-posthog-exist">every product that companies need</a> to run their business from their first day, to the day they IPO, and beyond. The operating system for folks who build software.</p> <p>We started with open-source product analytics, <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/story">launched out of Y Combinator's W20 cohort</a>. We've since shipped <a href="https://posthog.com/products">more than a dozen products</a>, including:</p> <ul> <li> <p><a href="https://posthog.com/docs/data-warehouse">A built-in data warehouse</a>, so users can query product and customer data together using custom SQL insights.</p> </li> <li> <p><a href="https://posthog.com/docs/cdp">A customer data platform</a>, so they can send their data wherever they need with ease.</p> </li> <li> <p><a href="https://posthog.com/ai">PostHog AI</a>, an AI-powered analyst that answers product questions, helps users find useful session recordings, and writes custom SQL queries.</p> </li> </ul> <p>Next on the roadmap are CRM, messaging, revenue analytics, and support products. When we say every product that companies need to run their business, we really mean it!</p> <p>We are:</p> <ol> <li> <p><strong>Product-led</strong>. More than 100,000 companies have installed PostHog, mostly driven by word-of-mouth. We have intensely strong product-market fit.</p> </li> <li> <p><a href="https://paulgraham.com/aord.html"><strong>Default alive</strong></a>. Revenue is growing 10% MoM on average, and we're very efficient. We raise money to push ambition and grow faster, not to keep the lights on.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Well-funded.</strong> We've raised more than $100m from some of the world's top <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/strategy/investors">investors</a>. We're set up for a long, ambitious journey.</p> </li> </ol> <p>We're focused on building an awesome product for end users, hiring exceptional teammates, shipping fast, and <a href="https://posthog.com/deskhog">being as weird as possible</a>.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><strong>Things we care about</strong></h2> <ul> <li> <p><strong>Transparency:</strong> Everyone can read about our roadmap, how we pay (or even let go of) people, our strategy, and how we work, in our <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook">public company handbook</a>. Internally, we share revenue, notes and slides from board meetings, and fundraising plans, so everyone has the context they need to make good decisions.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Autonomy:</strong> We don’t tell anyone what to do. Everyone chooses what to work on next based on what's going to have the biggest impact on our customers, and what they find interesting and motivating to work on. <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/wide-company">Engineers lead product teams</a> and <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/which-products">make product decisions</a>. Teams are flexible and easy to change when needed.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Shipping fast:</strong> <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/values#why-not-now">Why not now?</a> We want to build a lot of products; we can't do that shipping at a normal pace. We've built the company around small teams – autonomous, highly-efficient groups of <a href="https://posthog.com/founders/cracked-manifesto">cracked engineers</a> who can outship much larger companies because they own their products end-to-end.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Time for building:</strong> Nothing gets shipped in a meeting. We're a natively remote company. We default to async communication – PRs &gt; Issues &gt; Slack. Tuesdays and Thursdays are <a href="https://posthog.com/handbook/company/culture#were-on-the-makers-schedule">meeting-free days</a>, and we prioritize heads down building time over perfect coordination. This will be the most productive job you've ever had.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Ambition:</strong> We want to solve big problems. We strongly believe that aiming for the best possible upside, and sometimes missing, is better than never trying. We're optimistic about what's possible and our ability to get there.</p> </li> <li> <p><strong>Being weird:</strong> Weird means redesigning an already world-class website for the 5th time. It means shipping <em>literally</em> every product that relates to customer data. It means building an <a href="https://posthog.com/deskhog">objectively unnecessary developer toy</a> with dubious shareholder value. Doing weird stuff is a competitive advantage. And it's fun.</p> </li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><strong>Who we’re looking for</strong></h2> <ul> <li> <p>A customer-obsessed person to take care of a large number of our larger customers. You’ll engage with them regularly to ensure their continued retention and growth.</p> </li> <li> <p>You'll need to be incredibly helpful, technical enough to help o
